message:
fix(atc): fix e-process predictability violation and DR variance numerical stability

1. ConfidenceSequence::update: moved e-value update BEFORE lambda
   adaptation. The e-process validity guarantee (type-I error <= alpha
   under optional stopping) requires lambda_i to be F_{i-1}-measurable,
   meaning it can only depend on observations 1..i-1, not observation i.
   Previously lambda was adapted using Welford's update (which includes
   the current observation) and then applied to compute the e-value
   contribution for the same observation — a look-ahead bias that
   invalidates the sequential validity guarantee.

2. DR variance: clamped one-pass variance formula result to >= 0.0.
   The formula E[X^2] - (E[X])^2 can produce small negative values due
   to floating-point cancellation when DR terms are nearly identical.
   Negative variance is nonsensical and could confuse downstream consumers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
